name: context_brief
description: One-call session-start digest — call this FIRST in every new conversation instead of separate get_project/retrieve/task_list calls. Returns a token-budgeted briefing: identity (L4 user preferences), last_session (recent L2 summaries), project_knowledge (pinned checkpoints + top L3 facts), recent_changes (items stored in the last N days), recent_attempts (tried-and-failed records so you don't repeat dead ends), open_tasks, and stats (item counts by layer).

session_id.description: Project slug — scopes open_tasks and L1 session context

project.description: Project name filter for Qdrant-level isolation (optional)

max_tokens.description: Token budget for the whole briefing (default: 2000)

recent_days.description: Window for the recent_changes section in days (default: 7)

name: context_retrieve
description: Retrieve relevant context items for the current query from all memory layers.

session_id.description: Project namespace or session identifier. Pass the project slug (e.g. 'taty-v2') to scope retrieval to that project only and avoid cross-project contamination. Omit only for cross-project or global queries.

project.description: Project name to filter results by (e.g. 'taty-v2'). Filters at the Qdrant level for efficient project-scoped retrieval. Can be used alongside or instead of session_id.

memory_layers.description: Which memory layers to query (default: all)

expand_relations.description: Also include memories linked to the retrieved items via relations (1-hop, marked with via_relation). Default: false

name: context_store
description: Store content into the appropriate memory layer. Pass either a single 'content' + 'memory_layer', or 'items' to store several facts in one call (preferred when saving multiple facts — one round-trip instead of N). After storing (L2-L4), same-purpose detection runs automatically: near-identical memories are auto-linked with alternative_to edges (auto_linked in the response); probable matches are returned as suggestions to confirm with context_relate (suggestions in the response). To record a failed approach (so future sessions don't repeat it), store an L2 item with metadata {type: 'attempt', goal, approach, outcome: 'failed', why} — context_brief surfaces these in its recent_attempts section.

items.description: Batch mode: list of items to store in one call. Each item: {content, memory_layer, importance?, metadata?, confidence?, session_id?, origin?}. When provided, top-level content/memory_layer are ignored.

session_id.description: Project namespace or session identifier. Use the project slug (e.g. 'taty-v2') for project-specific facts so they are retrievable in isolation. Use a descriptive slug (e.g. 'user-preferences') for global or cross-project facts.

metadata.description: Extra metadata (optional). Conventions: files: [paths] — the files this fact refers to; their content hashes are stored so retrieval can flag the memory possibly_stale when they change. fact_type: 'file-location' | 'config' | 'architecture' | 'preference' — controls type-aware TTL decay in GC (file locations expire fast, preferences slowly). type: 'attempt' marks a tried-and-failed record (see description).

origin.description: Provenance of this fact: 'user-stated' when the user said it directly, 'agent-inferred' for your own conclusions (default), 'web-search' for facts found online. Ingested content is tagged automatically. Stored in metadata.origin and returned on retrieval so trust level is always visible.

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_summarize
description: Summarize the working memory for a session. Runs async, off the critical path.

focus.description: What to focus on in the summary (optional)

name: context_score
description: Score a list of context items by relevance to a query.

items.description: List of context items to score

name: context_checkpoint
description: Save a named checkpoint — a pinned, high-importance L3 memory snapshot of a decision, milestone, or code state. Use this when something significant happens that should be permanently remembered and retrievable by name.

content.description: What to snapshot — decision rationale, code summary, milestone description

project.description: Project name for filtering (optional)

session_id.description: Project namespace or session identifier. Use the project slug (e.g. 'taty-v2') to associate this checkpoint with a specific project.

name: context_deprecate
description: Mark an existing memory item as deprecated. The item is NOT deleted — it stays in the store but is excluded from normal retrieval. Use this when a checkpoint or fact is superseded by a newer one. Pass superseded_by to record which item replaces it (creates a 'supersedes' relation automatically).

item_id.description: ID of the item to deprecate

reason.description: Why this item is being deprecated (optional)

superseded_by.description: ID of the newer item that replaces this one — records a 'supersedes' relation (new item → deprecated item) so replacement history is traceable (optional)

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_relate
description: Link two memory items with a typed relation (source → target) so they can be retrieved and handled together. Known types: related_to, supersedes, part_of, depends_on, caused_by — custom types are also accepted.

source_id.description: ID of the source memory item

target_id.description: ID of the target memory item

relation_type.description: Relation type (default: related_to). Known: related_to, supersedes, part_of, depends_on, caused_by. Custom strings allowed.

project.description: Project name tag (optional)

name: context_unrelate
description: Remove a relation between two memory items — by relation_id, or by the source_id + target_id pair (optionally narrowed by relation_type).

relation_id.description: ID of the relation to remove

source_id.description: Source item ID (used with target_id when relation_id is not given)

target_id.description: Target item ID (used with source_id when relation_id is not given)

relation_type.description: Only remove edges of this type (optional, with source_id/target_id)

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_related
description: List the memories linked to an item via relations, with the connecting edges. Follows supersedes chains into deprecated items (direct lookup, not search).

item_id.description: Memory item ID whose neighbors to fetch

relation_type.description: Only follow edges of this type (optional)

direction.description: Edge direction relative to item_id (default: both)

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_get
description: Fetch a single memory item directly by its ID — no vector search. Works for deprecated items too. Use when you already know the item id (e.g. from a previous store/retrieve/related call).

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_visualize
description: Render the memory graph (items + relation edges) as a Mermaid flowchart. Nodes are colored by memory layer (L1-L4), deprecated items are dashed, pinned items get a thick border; edges are labeled with their relation type. The returned mermaid string renders natively in Claude and any Mermaid viewer. Best for up to ~50 nodes.

project.description: Filter items by project tag (optional)

memory_layer.description: Only include items from this layer (optional; L4 reads the shared user collection)

relations_only.description: Only show items that have at least one relation edge (default: false)

include_deprecated.description: Include deprecated items, e.g. supersedes targets (default: true)

direction.description: Graph layout direction: left-to-right or top-down (default: LR)

name: context_alternatives
description: Answer 'what can I use for X?' — semantic search for a purpose (e.g. 'approve button'), returning each matching memory grouped with its alternatives (items linked via alternative_to or used_for edges). Alternatives are detected automatically at store time: near-identical purposes are auto-linked, probable matches are returned as suggested_relations in the store response.

query.description: The purpose to search for, e.g. 'approve button component'

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_ingest
description: Parse and ingest any file or URL into memory as chunks. Supports .docx, .pdf, .md, source code files (.py, .js, .ts, .go, .rs, ...), and any http(s):// URL. Each chunk is embedded and stored automatically.

source.description: Absolute file path or URL to ingest

memory_layer.description: Memory layer to store chunks in (default: L3)

project.description: Project name tag for filtering (optional)

session_id.description: Project namespace. Always set this to the project name (e.g. 'taty-v2') so ingested chunks are scoped to that project and retrievable in isolation without cross-project contamination.

name: context_list
description: List memory items without a vector search — for browsing checkpoints, reviewing what's stored, or finding items to deprecate.

project.description: Filter by project name (optional)

name: context_task_add
description: Add a new task to remember for later. Use this when the user mentions something they want to do later, a feature to build, or any pending work item.

project.description: Project name for filtering (optional)

name: context_task_list
description: List pending or all tasks. Call this at the start of a session to see what work is waiting.

project.description: Filter by project (optional)

name: context_task_update
description: Update a task — change its status, priority, title, or description.

name: context_set_project
description: Set the active project for the current user. All subsequent memory operations (store, retrieve, list, ingest, checkpoint) will be scoped to a dedicated Qdrant collection for this project (e.g. 'synatyx' → collection 'ctx_synatyx'). The selection is persisted in Redis and survives server restarts. If unsure of the project name, call context_get_project first — it will suggest the workspace folder name.

project.description: Project name to activate (e.g. 'synatyx', 'taty-v2'). Will be slugified automatically.

name: context_get_project
description: Return the currently active project for the user. If no project has been set, returns a suggestion based on the current workspace folder name — call context_set_project with the suggested name to confirm.

name: context_skill_store
description: Save a skill definition. Writes full content to PostgreSQL and embeds only the description into Qdrant L3 for RAG matching.

description.description: One-line description used for RAG matching

project.description: Project scope (optional, null = global)

name: context_skill_find
description: RAG search for the best matching skill(s) for a given task. Embeds the query, searches Qdrant L3 filtered by type='skill', then fetches full content from PostgreSQL.

query.description: Task description to match against stored skills

project.description: Limit search to a specific project (optional)

top_k.description: Max number of skills to return (default: 3)

name: context_skill_get
description: Fetch a skill by exact name or slug from PostgreSQL.

name.description: Skill name or slug to look up

project.description: Project scope filter (optional)

name: context_skill_list
description: List all stored skills for the user, optionally scoped to a project.

project.description: Filter by project (optional)

name: context_skill_delete
description: Remove a skill from PostgreSQL and deprecate its Qdrant embedding.

name.description: Skill name or slug to delete

name: context_gc_stats
description: Return garbage collection statistics for the active project — how many items are expiring soon, already deprecated, pending hard delete, or protected.

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)

name: context_consolidate
description: Manually trigger sleep-style memory consolidation for the active project: clusters of similar episodic (L2) memories (cosine >= threshold, cluster size >= minimum) are merged into a single L3 fact; the originals are deprecated (never deleted) and linked to the merged item with supersedes edges. Attempt records, pinned items, and existing consolidations are never merged. The GC daemon also runs this automatically in the background when enabled.

project.description: Project slug to operate in — overrides the active-project pointer for this call. Pass it in multi-session setups so concurrent sessions in different projects don't route into each other's collections (optional)
